candidate_mechanism.title: Feature Extraction Detail

equations.feature_operator: Phi(x_t, A_t) = c_l * L(x_t) + c_k * K_t * S(x_t, A_t) + c_a * U_t - c_i * I_t
equations.full_update: f_{t+1} = (1 - l_f) * f_t + e_f * g_t * Phi(x_t, A_t) + b_g * b_region

interpretation.local_contrast: feature extraction starts from local contrast, not from a full symbolic code
interpretation.shared_reuse: current structure routes local evidence toward already reusable subspaces
interpretation.compatibility_gate: high-compatibility relations receive earlier feature reuse and cleaner lifting
interpretation.abstraction_lift: abstraction is a late reuse term built on top of routed local features
interpretation.interference: overlap and mismatch suppress fragile or conflicting candidate features
interpretation.full_update: fast features emerge from local contrast under structural routing, then get amplified or damped by compatibility and abstraction pressure

candidate_mechanism.core_claim: Feature extraction is not a pure feedforward readout. It is a compatibility-weighted, structure-routed process that begins with local contrast and grows into reusable abstractions.

project_readout.summary: P6C is positive only if the feature term can be expanded into a concrete extraction law, rather than treated as an opaque encoding operator.
project_readout.next_question: If P6C holds, the next stage should fuse P6B and P6C into one structure-feature co-evolution equation and start attacking remaining residuals directly.
